attacking_style: Attacks on final climbs to recover time or exploit emotional momentum, often immediately after teammate success or personal setbacks. Struggles to sustain efforts on gradients exceeding 10%.
team_tactics: Team prioritizes his GC time recovery over stage wins, even when teammates lead breakaways. Limited high-mountain domestique support forces opportunistic attacks rather than controlled setups.
terrain_preference: Suited to moderate gradient climbs where resilience and tactical timing matter. Exposed on steep (10%+) gradients and narrow finishing ramps where positioning and pure power determine outcomes.
vulnerability: Cracks on sustained 10%+ gradients. Vulnerable to positioning squeeze-outs on narrow, steep finishes. Must gamble on attacks to gain time rather than defend through team strength.
key_patterns: Attacks final climbs to recover GC time lost earlier in race (3 observations)
Team sacrifices stage opportunities to support his GC time recovery objectives (2 observations)
Loses significant time on sustained steep gradients 10% or steeper (2 observations)
Converts emotional/momentum moments into immediate attacks after crashes or teammate wins (2 observations)
Positioning disadvantage on narrow steep finishes costs opportunities versus strong climbers (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Recent 2023 data shows tactical competitiveness but positioning vulnerabilities persist; insufficient recent climbing performance data to assess current climbing capacity versus historical weakness on steep gradients
